title: InsideLLM Declarative Agent Manifest
description: Canonical manifest schema for InsideLLM declarative agents. v1.1 reconciles the v1 draft (docs/Agents-Plan.md) with Ultraplan v3 (docs/Platform-Ultraplan-v3.md §2.2). JSON is the wire format; YAML is supported for authoring and is round-trippable.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Pinned; translators refuse unknown versions.

agent_id.description: Immutable identifier, kebab-case, unique per tenant. Forking creates a new agent_id.

tenant_id.description: Which tenant / company this agent belongs to (e.g. 'example-tenant', 'parent-organization-acme').

created_by.description: Authoring user. Set at create time, retained on update for audit.

team.description: Owning team or department label. Used for default visibility.

icon.description: Icon name or URL. Small standard set preferred (e.g. 'shield-check', 'scale', 'gavel').

conversation_starters.description: Short example prompts shown to users to kick off a session.

instructions.description: System prompt / persona. Natural language. Treated as immutable at runtime — user prompts cannot override. Humility guardrail still layered on top.

knowledge.description: Scoped knowledge sources available to this agent.

collections.description: RAG collection names bound to this agent. Retrieval is server-side restricted to this list.

scope.description: strict = retrieve only from declared collections (recommended). loose = fall back to tenant-global collections on miss.

connectors.description: Live data connectors queried at retrieval time (not pre-embedded). Each entry references a governance_data_connector by name.

purpose.description: Short description of what this connector is for; shown in audit.

urls.description: Allowlisted URLs the agent may fetch (DLP-scanned on retrieval).

actions.description: Catalog actions the agent may invoke. Registration records live in governance_actions and declare their backend (FastAPI / Celery / n8n / Activepieces / MCP).

action_id.description: References an entry in the action catalog.

approval_required.description: When true, the action invocation is gated on an HITL approval before execution.

approval_target.description: Who approves: role name ('manager'), user email, or team. Ignored when approval_required=false.

approval_channel.description: Channel the approval request is dispatched on.

scope.description: Per-action privilege hint. Catalog-action registration has the authoritative scope; this is a manifest-level downgrade option only.

guardrails.description: Runtime safety and model constraints. All fields flow into OPA input.

profile.description: Named OPA policy bundle. Portfolio → company → agent inheritance applies: an agent profile can only tighten its parent policies, never loosen.

token_budget_per_session.description: Cumulative token cap across all LLM calls in one session.

daily_usd_budget.description: Per-user daily spend cap on this agent. Enforced via the agent's LiteLLM virtual key.

allowed_models.description: Model IDs (LiteLLM's naming) this agent may invoke. Intersected with the tenant's global allowlist.

dlp_overrides.description: Narrow, audited exceptions to tenant DLP policy. Each key defaults false. Setting true requires the agent's guardrail profile to explicitly permit it.

visibility.description: Who can see and invoke this agent.

scope.description: private = author only. team = members of `team` above. org = all users in tenant. fleet = all users across all tenants (Parent Portfolio portfolio-wide). scope=org requires tier-2+ approval via governance_changes. scope=fleet requires tier-3 approval.

available_to.description: Groups or user emails that can invoke (union with scope-based reach).

meta.description: Free-form operator metadata. Not interpreted by the runtime.
